original: Alfred, King of Wessex, is surprised by a nighttime attack in Chippenham, by the Great Viking Army led by Guthrum the Dane. Alfred escapes into the Somerset marshes.
Philip of Swabia becomes King of the Romans
Ferrand, Count of Flanders, freed from the Louvre after being held prisoner for 12 years by the French
Stephen Uros III becomes King of Serbia
French King Jean II introduces Order of the Star
Charles I of Bohemia is crowned with the Iron Crown of Lombardy
San Marco convent designed by architect Michelozzo, with an altarpiece by Fra Angelico is consecrated in the presence of Pope Eugene IV in Florence
Byzantine Emperor Constantine XI is crowned at Mistra
Emperor Frederick III becomes Archduke of Austria
The first Mass in the New World is celebrated at La Isabela, Hispaniola
Jews are expelled from Graz in Styria, Austria
Northern Hasburg Netherlands counties of Artois and Hainault and city Dowaai sign pro-Spanish and pro-Roman Catholic Union of Arras (Unie van Atrecht)
Pope Gregory XV forms Congregatio the Propagande Fide
Virginia orders half of its tobacco crop destroyed to support plunging prices and avoid an economic catastrophe, the first colony to order the destruction of crops
The Fifth Monarchists unsuccessfully attempt to seize control of London
First recorded boxing match is organized by Christopher Monck, 2nd Duke of Albemarle, between his butler and his butcher
Joseph I, later Holy Roman Emperor and son of Emperor Leopold I, becomes King of the Romans and King in Germany
The Committee of Inquiry on the South Sea Bubble publishes its findings
Bonnie Prince Charlies' Jacobite army draws close to Glasgow
Massachusetts slaves petition legislature for freedom
Battle of Jersey: Major Francis Peirson and his men defeat invading French troops
Turkey and Russia sign a treaty in Constantinople
Indiana state legislature approves development of the future capital city of Indianapolis
New England Anti-Slavery Society organizes (Boston)
2 day storm off Irish & English coast immortalized as the "Big Wind"
4,500 British & Indian troops leave Kabul, massacred before reaching India
Patent for reducing zinc ore granted to Samuel Wetherill, Penn
Florida troops seize Federal arsenal at Apalachicola
NYC mayor proposes NY become a free city, trading with North and South
The inauguration of the Musikverein (Vienna)
Pope Pius IX encyclical "On the Church in Armenia"
Record snow cover in Seattle - 120 cm
Ontario Rugby Football Union forms
Great Northern Railway connects Seattle with east coast
1st US women's 6-day bicycle race starts, Madison Square Garden
1st telephone message from a submerged submarine, by Simon Lake
Boers attack at Ladysmith, about 1,000 killed or injured
Dutch Press museum opens in Amsterdam
New Mexico becomes 47th state of the Union
Attempting to end hostilities in the Balkans, the London Peace Conference breaks down because Turkey refuses to cede Adrianpole, the Aegean island, and Crete
Stock brokerage firm of Merrill Lynch founded
The Iraqi Army is formed
-13] Conference of Cannes concerning German retribution payments
Francis Poulenc's ballet "Les Biches", choreographed by Bronislava Nijinska, and danced by the Ballets Russe, premieres in Monte Carlo
Kees Boeke opens 1st comprehensive school in Holland
US marines return to Nicaragua
Alexander I establishes a royal dictatorship in Yugoslavia
1st diesel engine automobile trip (in a Packard sedan) completed
Barbara Hanley becomes Canada's 1st woman mayor in Webbwood, Ontario
Mass execution of Poles, committed by Germans in the city of Poznań, Warthegau.
Pan American Airlines becomes the first commercial airline to complete a flight around the world; the plane "California Clipper" is rechristened "Pacific Clipper" after the feat
Vietnam holds its first ever general election
Pan American Airlines offers a round-the-world-ticket, the first commercial airline to do so
Ray Lindwall smashes 100 v England in MCG Test
Britain recognizes Communist government of China
Ganghwa massacre: Hundreds of South Korean communist sympathisers are slaughtered
Indianapolis Olympians beat Rochester Royals, 75-73 in NBA-record 6 overtimes; also the longest game in NBA history
Dutch passenger ships Willem Ruys and Oranje collide in the Red Sea near Port Sudan, seriously damaging both vessels without injuring the 1,750 passengers
The first Asian Socialist Conference opens in Burma
WKBN TV channel 27 in Youngstown, Ohio (CBS) begins broadcasting - without sound, until January 11
Federal court bars former Little League Commissioner Carl Stotz from forming a rival group
KGNS TV channel 8 in Laredo, TX (NBC/ABC) begins broadcasting
Yeshiva Kol Ya'ackov opens in Moscow Russia
Bollingen Prize for poetry awarded to E. E. Cummings
WIPR TV channel 6 in San Juan, PR (PBS) begins broadcasting
"Mutual of Omaha's Wild Kingdom" with Marlin Perkins begins on NBC
Charlie Finlay announces he wants to move KC A's to Louisville
Rolling Stones' 1st tour of England as headline act: The Ronettes and The Swinging Blue Jeans open for them
Geoff Boycott takes 3-47 against South Africa, his best Test bowling
KHTV TV channel 39 in Houston, TX (IND) begins broadcasting
Two homemade buses collide on a mountain road in Terpate, Philippines and plunge off a cliff, killing 84 people and injuring 140
United States Marine Corps and ARVN troops launch "Operation Deckhouse Five" in the Mekong River delta.
Dr Norman E. Shumway performs 1st US adult cardiac transplant operation
Surveyor 7 (last of series) launched by US for soft-landing on Moon
The Beatles' album "Magical Mystery Tour" goes #1 and stays #1 for 8 weeks
WLIW TV channel 21 in Garden City, NY (PBS) begins broadcasting

Australian boxer Johnny Famechon retains his WBC featherweight title with a 14th-round knockout of Fighting Harada in Tokyo; re-match of questionable 15-round decision in 1969 in his first title defense
Berkeley chemists announces 1st synthetic growth hormones
Cecil Partee elected president pro tem of Illinois state senate
Vladimir Bukovsky is exiled from USSR
"Schoolhouse Rock" premieres on ABC-TV with Multiplication Rock
"CBS Mystery Theater" premieres on radio
In response to the 1973 energy crisis, daylight saving time commences nearly four months early in the United States.
United Kingdom begins three-day work week during energy crisis
"AM America" premieres on ABC-TV with Bill Beutel as host
"Wheel Of Fortune" debuts on NBC-TV
A thousand Led Zeppelin fans, waiting overnight inside the lobby of the Boston Garden for tickets to the group's February 4th gig to go on sale, cause a riot and an estimated $30,000 damage
China performs nuclear test at Lop Nor, PRC
Charter 77, a document criticising the Czech government's human rights abuses, is published in Prague
1st postage stamp copyrighted by US (Carl Sandburg stamp)
US hand over St Stephan crown to Hungary
Shapour Bakhtiar government established by the Shah in Iran, will preside until unrest in the country subsides
Village People's "Y.M.C.A." becomes their only UK #1 single; at its peak, it sells over 150,000 copies per day
NHL Philadelphia Flyers set North American professional sports record of 35 straight games without a defeat (25-0-10)
The beginning of the first GPS epoch
50th hat trick in Islander history-John Tonelli scored 5 goals
Challenger moves to Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center (Florida) for mating of STS 41 B mission
Last day of Test cricket for Chappell, Marsh & Lillee
British Defense Secretary Michael Heseltine resigns after the 'Westland affair'
Impala Platinum fires 20,000 black mine workers in Johannesburg
STS 61-C scrubbed at T-31sec because of liquid oxygen valve problem
100th US Congress convenes
Astronomers at the University of California witness the first sight of the birth of a galaxy
NY Lotto pays $35 million to one winner (#s are 18-25-26-32-42-44)
"Real Life With Jane Pauley" premieres on NBC-TV
Jorge Serrano Elias elected President of Guatemala
Portland Trail Blazers beat the Seattle SuperSonics, 114-111 before a sellout crowd at the Portland Memorial Coliseum; marks the Blazers' 600th consecutive sellout at home
Qian Hong swims female world record 50m butterfly (27.30 sec)
NY Yankees sign free agent Danny Tartabul
Robert Schenkkan's series of plays "Kentucky Cycle" premieres in LA
The US Government urges doctors to stop using silicone breast implants, outlining the health effects
Zviad Gamskahurdia, the President of Georgia, flees the country because of a military coup
55 Kashmiri civilians are killed in Sopore, Jammu and Kashmir by Indian Border Security Force units
Jean Mueller discovers comet Mueller/1993a
Dow Jones Industrial Average hits record 3803.88
Chemical fire in an apartment complex in Manila, Philippines, leads to the discovery of plans for Project Bojinka, a mass-terrorist attack
Record £42 million ($65.2 million) British lottery won by 3 people (2-3-4-13-42-44)
Barry Switzer resigns as Dallas Cowboy coach
An agreement is reached by the NBA and the players union to end a 204-day lockout which shortened the season by 50 games
The flu outbreak in Britain puts pressure on NHS
The last Pyrenean ibex is found dead after being crushed by a tree
Daniel Bedingfield returns to No.1 on the UK singles chart for the second time with Gotta Get Thru This
Costas Simitis announces his resignation as president of the Panhellenic Socialist Movement in Greece
Mississippi Civil Rights Workers Murders: Edgar Ray Killen is arrested as a suspect for the 1964 murders of three Civil Rights workers.
Israel begins an assault on the Gaza Strip
A suicide bomber blows himself up at a police station in Damascus, Syria, killing 26 people and wounding 63
10 people are killed by a US drone attack in South Waziristan, Pakistan
The NHL reaches an agreement to end a 113 day lockout
"Star Wars: The Force Awakens" breaks North American box office record, passing the $760.5M taken by "Avatar"
North Korea states that they have successfully conducted their fourth nuclear test, saying it was a hydrogen bomb in a claim disputed by most international experts
Opera "Angel's Bone" by composer Du Yun and librettist Royce Vavrek premieres at the Prototype Festival, New York City (Pulitzer Prize for Music 2017)
Jon Gruden returns to the NFL as Oakland Raiders head coach after nearly a decade of broadcasting (ESPN Monday Night Football 2009-17)
Oil tanker Sanchi collides with a freighter off the coast of Shanghai with 32 probable deaths
Tennessee Titans quarterback Marcus Mariota scores a touchdown off own pass in 22-21 victory over Kansas City Chiefs, second NFL quarterback to do so
Malaysian king Sultan Muhammad V abdicates after two years of rule in historic first
Record $3.1 million (333.6 million yen) price for giant bluefin tuna at Tokyo's Toyosu fish market bought by sushi restaurant owner Kiyoshi Kimura
Crawford Steel executives Sid Spiegel and Gary Stern via holding company S & S Sportsco purchase the Montreal Alouettes from the Canadian Football League
More than 50 pro-democracy activists rounded up and arrested in Hong Kong
New Study discovers ancient Roman concrete is stronger than modern concrete because of the use of a hot mixing technique, with quicklime that self-healed any cracks over time
US teacher shot by her six-year-old student at Richneck Elementary School, Newport, Virginia, later hospitalised with gunshot wounds
Israeli army claims it has destroyed Hamas's command centre in northern Gaza and killed more than 8,000 militants
Large winter storm caused by a polar vortex moves east across the US from the central plains to the mid Atlantic, bringing 18 inches of snow to Kansas
US records its first person to die from bird flu, in southwest Louisiana
Cnut the Greatcrowned King of England in London by Lyfing, Archbishop of Canterbury
Harold II(Harold Godwinson) crowned King of England after the death of his brother-in-lawEdward the Confessor
Henry V is crownedKing of Germany
Construction begins on Portugal's Jerónimos Monastery, designed by architect Diogo de Boitaca to commemorate the return ofVasco da Gamafrom India
English court favoriteGeorge Villiersis made Earl of Buckingham byKing James I
The English Rump Parliament votes to putCharles Ion trial for treason and other "high crimes"
NaturalistsAlexander von HumboldtandAimé Bonplandreach Quito, Ecuador
Napoleonic Wars: Invasion of Cayenne, by combined British, Portuguese and colonial Brazilian forces, begins
US President-electFranklin Pierceand his family are involved in a train wreck in Massachusetts that kills his 11-year-old son Benny
US Congress begins investigating theCrédit Mobilierscandal, a fraud by theUnion Pacific Railroadand Crédit Mobilier of America in the construction of the first transcontinental railroad
`Abd-allah II of Harar opens the Battle of Chelenqo with an attack on the camp of the Shewan army of NegusMenelik II
The Washington National Cathedral is chartered by Congress. The charter is signed by PresidentBenjamin Harrison.
Cecil Rhodesresigns as premier of Cape colony
Maurice Ravel's "Miroirs" premieres in Paris
Maria Montessoriopens her 1st Montessori school, in Rome
Geophysicist and meteorologistAlfred Wegenerpresents his controversial theory of continental drift in a lecture at the Geological Association at the Senckenberg-Museum, Frankfurt
Mikhail Frunze replacesLeon Trotskyas People's Commissioner of Military and Native Affairs (Minister of Defence) asTrotskyandJoseph Stalinbattle for power in the aftermath ofVladimir Lenin's death
Mussoliniforms a cabinet composed entirely of Fascists in Italy
Paavo Nurmi, sets indoor record, 4:13.6 mile & 14:44.6 5,000m
PopePius XIpublishes encyclical Mortalium animos (against oecumene)
Mother Teresaarrives in Calcutta to begin her work amongst India's poorest
Australian cricket iconDon Bradmanscores 452 not out for New South Wales v Queensland in Sydney; then world record individual score in a first-class match; runs scored in 415 minutes
Thomas Edisonsubmits his last patent application for an electroplating component - just two days before his death
Clyde Barrowkills Tarrant County Deputy Sheriff Malcolm Davis after walking into a trap set for another criminal
Bronze memorial statue of explorerHenry Hudsonerected in the Bronx, New York
MLB pitcherBob Fellerreports for duty in Norfolk, Virginia, after enlisting in the US Navy
PopePius XIIpublishes encyclical Quemadmodum
Elvis Presleymakes his 7th & final appearance on "TheEd SullivanShow"
"Milton BerleShow" last airs on ABC-TV
The Supremesrelease single "I'm Livin' In Shame"
Neil Youngreturns to his homeland of Canada for his first concert there since his pre-stardom days, performing at the Queen Elizabeth Theatre in Vancouver, British Columbia
Ted Turnerpurchases Atlanta Braves for reported $12 million
EMI record label drop punk rock groupSex Pistols
Indira Gandhi's Congress Party wins elections in India
BassistBill Wymanannounces he will leave the band the Rolling Stones
US figure skating championNancy Kerriganis attacked byTonya Harding's bodyguard at the US Championships in Detroit, Michigan
Atlanta Hawks' coachLenny Wilkenspasses the legendary Red Auerbach to become the NBA's all-time winningest coach, with 939 career victories; Hawks beat Washington, 112-90 at the Omni
Don Suttonselected to Baseball Hall of Fame
Thierry Henryre-signs for Arsenal on a two-month loan deal
Truce begins between Free Syrian Army and forces loyal to PresidentBashar al-Assadin northern Damascus
Randy Johnson,Pedro Martínez, John Smoltz and Craig Biggio are inducted into the Baseball Hall of Fame
Governor of CaliforniaJerry Browndeclares state of emergency over methane gas leak on outskirts of Los Angeles
Elizabeth Warrenannounces her intention to run for a second term as Massachusetts Senator
North Korean leaderKim Jong-unsays country's five-year economic plan has failed at opening of a rare meeting of the Workers' Party

Supporters of US PresidentDonald Trumpstorm the Capitol in Washington during the congressional certification ofJoe BidenandKamala Harris's win, resulting in five deaths and prompting evacuation of lawmakers and Vice PresidentMike Pence
